metadata.description: Core HRF basis combination functionality with metadata handling and matrix operations

semantic_description.purpose: Validate the bind_basis function that combines multiple HRF objects into unified multi-basis HRF
semantic_description.algorithm:
The bind_basis function is fundamental to HRF system architecture, enabling combination of multiple HRFs:
**Core Algorithm:** 1. Accept multiple HRF objects as input 2. Validate that all inputs are HRF objects 3. Handle single HRF case (return as-is) 4. Calculate combined metadata: - combined_nbasis = sum of individual nbasis values - combined_span = max of individual span values - combined_name = concatenated names with " + " separator 5. Create combined function that: - Evaluates each input HRF at time t - Column-binds (cbind) the results into matrix 6. Return new HRF object using as_hrf with combined attributes
**Matrix Combination:** - Single-basis HRFs: column vectors become matrix columns - Multi-basis HRFs: matrices are column-concatenated - Final matrix: rows=time points, columns=total basis functions
**Metadata Rules:** - nbasis: Additive (sum of all input nbasis values) - span: Maximum (longest temporal window) - name: Concatenative (joined with " + ") - params: Empty list (individual params don't combine meaningfully)
semantic_description.mathematical_properties:
**Combination Properties:** - Linear combination: Combined HRF is linear combination of inputs - Basis preservation: Each input HRF becomes subset of output columns - Temporal alignment: All HRFs evaluated at same time points - Dimension consistency: Output columns = sum of input basis counts
**Matrix Structure:** - Block structure: [HRF1 | HRF2 | ... | HRFn] - Column ordering preserves input order - Each block has width = nbasis of corresponding input HRF - No interaction between blocks (simple concatenation)
**Attribute Inheritance:** - Combined span accommodates all input HRFs - Combined nbasis enables complete basis set - Name reflects all contributing HRFs - Class remains "HRF" and "function"
semantic_description.edge_cases: - Single HRF input: Should return input unchanged - Empty input: Should trigger error - Non-HRF inputs: Should trigger validation error - Mixed single/multi basis HRFs: Should handle correctly - Different span values: Should use maximum - Very long names: Should concatenate properly

input.name: mixed_basis_hrfs
input.description: Mix of single and multi-basis HRFs

check.location: max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 1] - HRF_SPMG1(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 2] - HRF_GAMMA(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 3] - HRF_GAUSSIAN(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_GAMMA)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 1] - HRF_SPMG1(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_GAMMA)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 2:3] - HRF_SPMG2(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_GAMMA)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))[, 4] - HRF_GAMMA(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5))))
ncol(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_SPMG3, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)(seq(0, 20, by = 1)))
max(abs(bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA)(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5)) - cbind(HRF_SPMG1(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5)), HRF_GAMMA(seq(0, 20, by = 0.5)))))

implementations.R:
# R reference implementation # Function: bind_basis(...) # Location: R/hrf.R lines 48-76
# Test single HRF (should return unchanged) single_result <- bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1)
# Test three single-basis HRFs triple_single <- bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)
# Test mixed single and multi-basis mixed_result <- bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_GAMMA)
# Test large combination large_combo <- bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_SPMG2, HRF_SPMG3, HRF_GAMMA, HRF_GAUSSIAN)
# Test evaluation time_vec <- seq(0, 20, by = 0.5) eval_triple <- triple_single(time_vec) eval_mixed <- mixed_result(time_vec)
# Test attribute extraction nbasis_triple <- nbasis(triple_single) span_triple <- attr(triple_single, "span") name_triple <- attr(triple_single, "name")
# Test consistency with manual cbind manual_combo <- cbind(HRF_SPMG1(time_vec), HRF_GAMMA(time_vec)) auto_combo <- bind_basis(HRF_SPMG1, HRF_GAMMA)(time_vec)
